notes: Integrated from the real Grade 2 Quarter 2, Book 11 source file.
Strong Reader card: longer text and more inferential fable work.

book.title: The Cat and the Mice
book.description: The Cat and the Mice includes a six-chapter reading sequence, vocabulary, comprehension questions, journal/writing prompts, and assessment checks.

choiceGuidance.reason: longer text and more inferential fable work

weeks.title: The Cat and the Mice Book Card
reading: Chapter 1: A House Full of Mice
Chapter 2: A New and Dangerous Resident
Chapter 6: The Difference Between Ideas and Action
vocabulary: 1. predator - an animal that hunts and eats other animals
2. dominance - power and control over others
1. drastic - extreme and likely to cause big changes
1. implemented - put into action; carried out
2. limitations - restrictions or weaknesses that prevent something from working
journal: Write advice to the mice about making safe choices.
Explain the message of the fable in your own words.
writing: Write advice to the mice about making safe choices.
Explain the message of the fable in your own words.
questions: 1. How had the mice organized themselves in the house?
2. How did the mice feel about their situation?
1. Why did the house owners bring in the cat?
2. How did the cat's arrival change life for the mice?
1. Why did the mice call an emergency council meeting?
2. What did the eldest mouse say about this cat?
1. What was the young mouse's solution?
2. How did the other mice react to this proposal?
1. What important question did the old mouse ask?
2. How did the mice react to this question?
1. Why couldn't the mice carry out their plan?
2. What lesson did the mice learn?
assessment: Use chapter comprehension questions for daily checks.
Use journal/writing prompt as written-response evidence.
